title: mimusops coriacea
intro: Mimusops coriacea, also known as the West African boxwood or boxwood tree, is a species of evergreen tree in the family Sapotaceae. The bark and roots of this plant have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Mimusops coriacea include treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as being used as a natural remedy for wounds and infections. In herbal preparations, the bark and roots of the plant are often dried and ground into a powder or made into a decoction, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
